description: This figure presents engineering stress-strain curves of ferritic and ferrite-based duplex low-density Fe-Mn-Al-C steels after different heat treatments. The tested alloys include two ferritic steels (Fe-6.8Al, Fe-8.1Al, annealed at 850°C for 5min, from reference [41]), one ferrite-based duplex steel Fe-3.3Mn-6.6Al-0.2C treated by 800°C/5min annealing and 800°C/5min + 400°C/10min austempering (from reference [44]), and another duplex steel Fe-8.5Mn-5.6Al-0.3C treated at 800°C for 1min, 800°C for 30min, and 900°C for 30min (from reference [51]). The y-axis is engineering stress in MPa, and x-axis is engineering strain in %. Ferritic steels show ultimate tensile strength (UTS) of 450-520 MPa with total elongation (TE) of 15-30%. The duplex steels exhibit wider performance ranges: Fe-8.5Mn-5.6Al-0.3C annealed at 900°C for 30min delivers the best strength-ductility balance with UTS ~720 MPa and TE ~75%, and austempered Fe-3.3Mn-6.6Al-0.2C shows higher elongation than directly annealed counterparts. This figure supports that composition and heat treatment adjustment can tune mechanical properties of low-density steels in a wide range, and ferrite-based duplex steels achieve excellent mechanical performance via TRIP/TWIP effects.
